behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is classification/counting logic: one rename event must map to exactly one category. The behavior text is about category semantics ("appear in their own category" and "not also counted"), not OS/network/subprocess behavior. System was considered and rejected because this wording does not require real git process behavior to expose the core bug; a parser/normalizer fixture with rename records is sufficient. Integration was considered and rejected because no explicit cross-module handoff/order/state propagation is required by this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative change records (including rename records such as R100 old->new) into the categorization/counting function and assert: `renamed == 1`, `added == 0`, `deleted == 0` for that event, and totals remain consistent when mixed with true add/delete/modify records.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test can run entirely in-process with no real git repo, subprocess, or filesystem interactions.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often appears at subprocess/real-git boundary: binary diffs can produce special output forms and exit/encoding behaviors that mocks sanitize away. `REAL-INFRA` applies because behavior references actual diffs containing binary files and crash resistance during real CLI execution. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text: no AI/LLM/model step is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary git repo, commit baseline, add/modify at least one real binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI as a subprocess, assert process exits successfully, and assert report includes binary file in the correct counted category.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real git subprocess behavior with an actual repository and binary content to catch crash modes hidden by stubs.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Unborn-HEAD behavior is a real git/process edge case (exit code, stderr text, command semantics) that is easy to mis-handle if only mocked. `REAL-INFRA` and `MOCK-HIDE` both point to System. Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior contains no model-dependent decision or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repository with zero commits, invoke the CLI subprocess against it, assert zero/non-error exit status, and assert stdout is a valid empty structured report (not traceback/error text).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs real repository state with no commits and real subprocess execution to verify true failure boundary.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against actual repository history; failure can arise from git command usage, parsing, and aggregation together. Behavior text explicitly ties correctness to the real repository being analyzed, so demoting to pure in-process integration risks missing real git-output mismatches. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no AI/LLM dependency in behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a temp repo with a controlled sequence of commits containing known adds/deletes/renames/modifications, run the CLI subprocess, and assert reported counts exactly match expected totals derived from the scripted history.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System-level test is required to validate interaction with real git history and subprocess output format.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics are git index/working-tree behaviors at real tool boundary; mocks can hide subtle differences in command flags and plumbing output. `REAL-INFRA` favors System because correctness depends on true git state transitions. Agentic/Workflow rejected by behavior text since no LLM/model steps exist.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent logical changes in two scenarios (staged only vs unstaged only), run the CLI subprocess for each scenario, normalize nondeterministic fields if any (e.g., timestamps), and assert outputs are identical.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real index/working-tree transitions and subprocess execution; lower-level fakes may miss state-dependent divergences.
